---
title: "GUDID Explorer"
---

An interactive explorer for the GUDID medical device dataset, built with [DYF](https://github.com/jdonaldson/dyf).

## What's here

- **[Categories](categories/index.qmd)** — Browse all product categories organized by functional group
- **Chat** — Ask questions about the dataset using an in-browser LLM (toggle the chat panel on the right)

## About the dataset

This explorer presents a structured view of medical device records from the FDA's [Global Unique Device Identification Database (GUDID)](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov/). Devices are organized into semantically coherent categories using DYF's tree-based clustering and Louvain community detection on embedding centroids.

Each category page shows:

- A description of what the category contains
- Sample products from the category
- Related categories (by embedding similarity and bundle membership)

The chat panel runs entirely in your browser — no server required. It loads a small LLM (Qwen 1.5B), a text-to-speech model, and an embedding model for semantic search.

::: {.callout-tip}
## First visit?
The chat panel needs to download ~1.1GB of model weights on first visit. They're cached by your browser for fast subsequent loads.
:::
